title: crocus × koritnicus
intro: Crocus × koritnicus, also known as the Greek crocus, is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the iris family, derived from the cross between Crocus cartwrightianus and Crocus pallasii. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate circulation, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The health benefits of Crocus × koritnicus include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its capacity to lower blood pressure and improve cognitive function. Traditionally, the plant's bulbs are used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although further research is necessary to confirm its full range of effects.
